file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
text_en: If you have a severe problem with the aortic valve in your heart, your doctor may recommend a transcatheter aortic valve implantation or tovy. It's also known as transcatheter aortic valve replacement or tover.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
text_en: Your aortic valve is one of the four valves in your heart. It lies between your left ventricle or lower left chamber and the large blood vessel supplying oxygen-rich blood to your body called the aorta.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
text_en: When your left ventricle contracts during each heartbeat, it pushes blood through three flexible cup-like leaflets that make up your aortic valve.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
text_en: When your left ventricle relaxes, your aortic valve closes to prevent blood from flowing back into your left ventricle.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
text_en: As you get older, your aortic valve may develop deposits of calcium that cause your leaflets to get thicker and less flexible. In this condition, your leaflets may not open fully. This condition, called calcific aortic valve stenosis, creates a narrowed opening that reduces the amount of blood flowing into your aorta and out to your body.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
text_en: As a result, the wall of your left ventricle thickens because it has to work harder to pump blood through the narrowed valve opening.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
text_en: At the beginning of your procedure, your doctor will make a small incision in your groin. After creating an opening in a blood vessel, called the femoral artery, your doctor will insert a flexible tube called an introducer sheath.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
text_en: Through the sheath, your doctor will insert a flexible guide wire into your femoral artery. The guide wire will be passed through your femoral artery all the way up to your aorta. Then the guide wire will be pushed through the opening in your aortic valve and into your left ventricle.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
text_en: Your doctor will use the guide wire to pass a flexible tube called a catheter through your aortic valve. A balloon on the tip of the catheter will be inflated to widen the opening in your aortic valve to push the valve leaflets to the sides.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
text_en: After deflating the balloon, your doctor will remove the catheter.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
text_en: The replacement valve consists of a tissue valve surrounded by a wire mesh called a stent. The replacement valve will be compressed and placed on the tip of another catheter.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
text_en: This catheter will be inserted through your aortic valve. When the catheter reaches the opening in your aortic valve, your doctor will inflate a balloon underneath the replacement valve to expand it and the stent.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
text_en: After placement of the new aortic valve, your doctor will deflate the balloon. The stent will support and secure the replacement valve in place.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
text_en: The catheter and guide wire will be removed.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
text_en: At the end of the procedure, the introducer sheath in your groin will be removed and your skin incision will be closed. You will have another incision on the other side of your groin through which instruments were inserted to monitor your heart during the procedure. This skin incision will also be closed.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
text_en:
To find out more about trans catheter aortic valve implantation, talk to your healthcare practitioner.

---

**切分说明（16 个 logic blocks）：**

| # | 主题 | 画面动作 |
|---|------|----------|
| 1 | 手术介绍 | 标题/概念引入 TAVI/TAVR |
| 2 | 主动脉瓣解剖 | 展示心脏四瓣膜、左心室与主动脉位置 |
| 3 | 瓣膜开放 | 左心室收缩，血液通过三个瓣叶 |
| 4 | 瓣膜关闭 | 左心室舒张，瓣膜关闭防止回流 |
| 5 | 钙化狭窄病变 | 钙沉积→瓣叶增厚→开口狭窄→血流减少 |
| 6 | 左室壁增厚 | 代偿性心肌肥厚动画 |
| 7 | 腹股沟切口+鞘管置入 | 切皮→股动脉开口→置入引导鞘 |
| 8 | 导丝推进 | 导丝经股动脉→主动脉→穿过瓣膜→进入左室 |
| 9 | 球囊扩张（预扩） | 导管通过瓣膜，球囊充气撑开瓣叶 |
| 10 | 球囊回收 | 球囊放气，撤出导管 |
| 11 | 人工瓣膜描述 | 展示组织瓣+金属支架结构，压缩装载 |
| 12 | 瓣膜释放 | 导管送入瓣膜位置，球囊扩张释放支架瓣 |
| 13 | 释放后固定 | 球囊放气，支架支撑瓣膜就位 |
| 14 | 撤出导管导丝 | 移除catheter和guide wire |
| 15 | 切口关闭 | 撤鞘管，缝合双侧腹股沟切口 |
| 16 | 结束语 | 建议咨询医生 |
